scenarios.description: children() on element with zero child nodes returns empty Elements (childNodeSize == 0 branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() filters out non-Element child nodes and returns empty when only TextNode children exist (childNodeSize > 0 and no element children)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("p"); el.appendText("text"); // one TextNode only

scenarios.description: children() returns single child Element when one element child present (childNodeSize > 0, one Element iteration yields one)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("ul"); Element li = parent.appendElement("li"); // single element child
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains exactly the appended child element at index 0

scenarios.description: children() returns multiple child Elements in document order when mixed Node children present (filters and preserves order)
scenarios.precondition: Element div = new Element("div"); div.appendChild(new TextNode("a")); Element e1 = div.appendElement("span"); div.appendText("b"); Element e2 = div.appendElement("em");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains [e1, e2] in order

scenarios.description: children() returns fresh Elements list each call, independent of cache (calling twice yields distinct Collections)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("ol"); Element first = parent.appendElement("li"); // ensure attributes.userData used for cache; then modify children
scenarios.expectedResult: first call returns [first], after parent.appendElement("li"), second call returns [first, second]
